title: 8 Natural Herbal Tinctures for Overactive Bladder and Urinary Incontinence

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid, also known as cranberry, may be good for overactive bladder because it has been shown to reduce the risk of urinary tract infections and inflammation, which can contribute to bladder problems.
Valeranone is good for overactive bladder because it helps to relax the muscles in the bladder and improve the flow of urine, reducing the need to urinate frequently.

Valerenal, also known as the bioactive compound found in cranberries, is good for overactive bladder because it has been shown to help reduce the frequency of urination and alleviate symptoms by preventing bacteria from sticking to the bladder walls.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, is a plant-based remedy that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The tincture made from this plant is known to have a soothing effect on the nervous system. For people with overactive bladder, valeriana officinalis tincture can be helpful because it can calm the muscles in the bladder and help to regulate the urge to urinate. This is achieved by reducing the activity of the bladder muscles and also by promoting relaxation, which can decrease the frequency of urination. The calming effect of valeriana officinalis tincture can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of overactive bladder. Additionally, this tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe any irritation or inflammation in the bladder, further reducing symptoms of overactive bladder. By taking valeriana officinalis tincture, individuals may experience fewer episodes of overactive bladder, reduced urgency, and improved bladder control.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon (also known as cranberry) herbal tincture that helps with overactive bladder, first you need to gather some fresh or dried cranberry fruit. You can use either of them, but fresh ones are more potent. Next, add the cranberries to a clean glass jar, making sure to cover them completely with 80-100 proof vodka or high-proof brandy. The ratio is usually 1 part of cranberries to 2-3 parts of the liquor. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily. After this period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Now you have your cranberry tincture, which you can store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. To use it, mix a few drops with water and take it before meals to help with overactive bladder symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for overactive bladder

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for overactive bladder because it helps relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce inflammation, allowing people to control their urination better.

Tannins, also known as cranberry, are good for overactive bladder because they may help prevent bacteria from sticking to the bladder walls, reducing the risk of infection and associated symptoms such as urgency and frequency.
Kaempferol, also known as cranberry, is good for overactive bladder because it helps prevent bacteria from sticking to the bladder walls, reducing the need to urinate frequently.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna tincture is a herbal remedy made from the hawthorn tree. It's often used for urinary issues because of its ability to relax the muscles in the bladder and urinary tract. When you have an overactive bladder, these muscles contract too much, leading to sudden and strong urges to urinate. Crataegus monogyna tincture may help calm these muscles, reducing the frequency of urination and the sensation of urgency. The tincture's active compounds, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, are thought to contribute to its relaxing effect on the muscles. This can be especially helpful for people who experience incontinence or wetting themselves due to an overactive bladder. Some studies suggest that Crataegus monogyna tincture may be effective in reducing symptoms of overactive bladder, but more research is needed to confirm its benefits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tincture, which may help with overactive bladder, you'll need fresh or dried cranberries (Vaccinium macrocarpon), a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a dark glass bottle for storing the final product. Start by preparing the cranberries: if using fresh cranberries, rinse them with water and pat them dry; if using dried cranberries, simply use them as is. Place the cranberries in the glass jar, and pour enough vodka to cover them completely. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the cranberry essence mix with the vodka. This process is called maceration. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids, and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. The recommended dosage for this tincture is typically 20-30 drops, taken 2-3 times a day. However, please note that before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're dealing with a health issue, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional or a licensed herbalist to ensure the tincture is safe and effective for your specific needs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a plant-based remedy that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. The tincture made from this plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. When it comes to overactive bladder, Hypericum perforatum tincture may help in several ways. It may relax the muscles in the bladder and the urinary tract, reducing spasms and allowing for more controlled urination. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may help protect the bladder from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bladder problems. Some people also use Hypericum perforatum tincture to help with anxiety and stress, which can sometimes be a trigger for overactive bladder. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing stress levels, the tincture may help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tincture that helps with overactive bladder, first you need to gather the right ingredients. Vaccinium macrocarpon, also known as cranberry, can be used to make the tincture. You will need fresh or dried cranberries, a high-proof vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar with a lid. Start by combining 1 cup of fresh or dried cranberries with 2 cups of vodka or glycerin in the glass jar, making sure the cranberries are fully covered by the liquid. Next, seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the mixture infuse. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. When you're ready to use the tincture, add a few drops to water and drink it, following the recommended dosage of 20-30 drops per day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the tincture, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata tincture is a herbal remedy that may help with overactive bladder problems. This plant, also known as 'locust bean', has been used in traditional medicine for a long time. The tincture is made from the leaves and flowers of the Cassia auriculata plant, which are rich in active compounds that may help calm the muscles in the bladder and urinary tract. When these muscles become too active, it can lead to frequent or urgent urination, and even incontinence. Cassia auriculata tincture may help relax these muscles, reducing the symptoms of overactive bladder and making it easier to control urination. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe any irritation or swelling in the bladder and urinary tract, further reducing discomfort and pain. Some people have found that using Cassia auriculata tincture as a natural remedy helps them manage overactive bladder and live a more comfortable life.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tincture for overactive bladder, start by preparing the ingredients. You'll need fresh or dried Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, which are also known as cranberries, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Begin by measuring the correct ratio, which is typically one part of dried berries to two parts of solvent. If using fresh berries, you'll need to dry them first by spreading them out in a single layer on a baking sheet and placing them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees Fahrenheit for a few hours. Once your berries are ready, combine them with the solvent in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the active compounds in the berries to infuse into the solvent. After the steeping time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture is now ready to use, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming it, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Cassia auriculata herbal tinctures for overactive bladder

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, is a plant that has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties. Its tincture, made from the plant's flowers, has been shown to be effective in treating overactive bladder (OAB) symptoms. This is because Lavandula angustifolia has a calming effect on the body, which helps to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination. The tincture works by soothing the muscles in the bladder and the nerves that control the bladder, making it easier to control the urge to urinate. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate discomfort associated with OAB. By reducing symptoms and improving quality of life, Lavandula angustifolia tincture can be a valuable natural remedy for individuals suffering from overactive bladder. It's often used in combination with other herbal remedies or under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures that help with overactive bladder, you'll need fresh or dried cranberries, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar. Start by cleaning and preparing the cranberries - you can use fresh or dried ones, but dried ones are more convenient. Then, fill a clean glass jar with the cranberries, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Next, pour the solvent over the cranberries, making sure they are completely covered. The general ratio for making a tincture is 1 part herbs to 2-3 parts solvent, so adjust the amount of solvent accordingly. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the beneficial compounds from the cranberries. After 2-4 weeks, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small doses, usually 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus tincture is a herbal remedy that may help people with overactive bladder. This condition causes a sudden need to urinate, often with little warning. The tincture is made from the root of the Ruscus aculeatus plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for many years. It works by relaxing the muscles in the bladder and helping to control sudden urges to urinate. The tincture also helps to improve the function of the nerves that control the bladder, which can become damaged over time. By reducing muscle spasms and improving nerve function, Ruscus aculeatus tincture may help people with overactive bladder to feel more in control and confident.  The tincture may also help to reduce the frequency of urination, which is a common symptom of overactive bladder. This can be especially helpful at night, when people with this condition often wake up to use the bathroom. By reducing the need to get up during the night, Ruscus aculeatus tincture may help people with overactive bladder to get a good night's sleep and feel more rested in the morning.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures for overactive bladder, start by gathering fresh or dried cranberries, which is the Latin name for Vaccinium macrocarpon. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid, a sharp knife, and a measuring cup. Begin by chopping the fresh cranberries into small pieces or crushing dried cranberries to release their herbal properties. Next, combine one part of the chopped cranberries with one part of a solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, in the glass jar. The general ratio is 1:1, but you can adjust it according to your preference. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar regularly to help the herbal properties infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa tinctures may help with overactive bladder. This is because the active compounds in Cannabis sativa, like cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), interact with the body's cannabinoid system. This system helps to regulate muscle control and spasms, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. When taken orally, CBD and THC may relax the muscles in the bladder and surrounding area, reducing the urge to urinate frequently. Additionally, Cannabis sativa tinctures may also help to reduce inflammation in the bladder, which can cause discomfort and pain. Some users have reported that taking Cannabis sativa tinctures helps to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, and may even help to reduce the risk of accidents or leaks. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of Cannabis sativa tinctures on overactive bladder.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon tinctures, also known as cranberry, are used to help people with overactive bladder. The tincture is made from the fruit of the cranberry plant, which has been used for centuries to help with urinary issues. The active ingredients in cranberry are thought to prevent bacteria from sticking to the bladder and urinary tract, which can cause bladder irritation and spasms. This can help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, making it easier for people with overactive bladder to manage their symptoms. Additionally, cranberry tinctures may help reduce inflammation and pain in the bladder, which can also contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. Some studies have suggested that cranberry supplements, like the tincture, may be effective in reducing symptoms of overactive bladder in some people. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and understand how it works.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures for overactive bladder, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, gather a handful of fresh or dried Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, also known as cranberries, and chop them into small pieces. Next, combine the chopped berries with 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, making sure that the berries are completely covered by the liquid. The ratio of berries to liquid is typically 1:2 or 1:3, meaning one part berries to two or three parts liquid. Secure the lid on the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar gently every day for 2-6 weeks to allow the berries to infuse their medicinal properties into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. This resulting liquid is your Vaccinium macrocarpon tincture, which can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water as needed to help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures for overactive bladder

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful solution for people who suffer from overactive bladder. One of the main reasons is that some herbs have a calming effect on the bladder muscles, reducing the urge to urinate frequently. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that can help to calm the nervous system and reduce bladder spasms. This can lead to fewer trips to the bathroom and a better quality of life. Crataegus monogyna, or hawthorn, has been shown to help improve blood flow to the bladder and surrounding muscles, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By using herbal tinctures containing these and other herbs, people with overactive bladder can experience relief from the constant discomfort and anxiety of needing to urinate often. Fixing this issue can greatly improve daily life, allowing for more freedom and spontaneity. No longer will you have to constantly worry about finding a bathroom, or rush to the toilet at a moment's notice. With herbal tinctures, you can regain control over your bladder and live a more normal life.
supplementary_best: A combination of Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Ruscus aculeatus tinctures may help remedy overactive bladder. Valeriana officinalis tincture is known for its calming and relaxing properties, which can help reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and improve urinary control. Lavandula angustifolia tincture has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the bladder muscles, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Ruscus aculeatus tincture has a direct effect on the bladder muscles, reducing contractions and spasms, and improving bladder tone.  This combination is effective because Valeriana officinalis and Lavandula angustifolia tinctures work together to calm the bladder muscles and reduce inflammation, while Ruscus aculeatus tincture provides direct relief from bladder spasms. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis and Lavandula angustifolia tinctures may also help with stress and anxiety, which can contribute to overactive bladder. By combining these three herbs, the tinctures work together to address the physical and emotional aspects of overactive bladder, providing a comprehensive remedy.  To use this combination, take 10-20 drops of each tincture, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water. The tinctures can be taken alone or in combination with other herbal remedies, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
supplementary_avoid: If you have overactive bladder, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can make bladder problems worse. One such herb is Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis). It has compounds that can slow down the emptying of the bladder, which can worsen overactive bladder symptoms. Another herb to avoid is Barberry (Berberis vulgaris), which also contains compounds that can slow down bladder emptying.  You should also limit or avoid tinctures made from Ginger (Zingiber officinale) and Turmeric (Curcuma longa) as they can increase urine production and worsen overactive bladder symptoms.  Additionally, tinctures made from Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) and Juniper Berry (Juniperus communis) should be used with caution as they can also increase urine production and worsen bladder control.  It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal tinctures if you have overactive bladder.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some natural remedies that can help with overactive bladder are probiotics and omega-3 fatty acids. Probiotics are good bacteria that live in our bodies and help keep our digestive system healthy. When we have an imbalance of good and bad bacteria, it can affect our bladder and cause problems like overactive bladder. Taking probiotics can help restore the balance and reduce symptoms. Omega-3 fatty acids are healthy fats that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder. They also help to relax the muscles in the bladder and make it less likely to contract too often. These natural remedies can be found in foods like yogurt, kefir, and fatty fish, or taken as supplements after consulting a doctor. Additionally, staying hydrated by drinking plenty of water can also help to dilute the urine and make it easier to pass, reducing the frequency of urination. This is because when the body is dehydrated, the urine is more concentrated and can irritate the bladder, making it more likely to contract too often. Drinking water can help to reduce this irritation and alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with overactive bladder (OAB) often experience other health issues that can be managed with medicinal herbs. One common condition is urinary tract infections (UTIs). UTIs occur when bacteria enter the urinary tract and cause inflammation. Medicinal herbs like uva ursi, juniper berries, and marshmallow root have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the urinary tract and prevent UTIs. Uva ursi, in particular, has antimicrobial properties that can fight off bacterial infections.  Another condition associated with OAB is constipation. Constipation can put extra pressure on the bladder, making symptoms worse. Herbs like senna, dandelion root, and peppermint can help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. Senna, for example, is a natural laxative that can help move stool through the digestive system.  Some people with OAB may also experience stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate symptoms. Herbs like chamomile, lavender, and passionflower have calming effects that can help reduce stress and promote relaxation. Chamomile, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the bladder and urinary tract.  People with OAB may also experience kidney stones, which can cause severe pain and discomfort. Herbs like stone root and juniper berries can help reduce inflammation and promote the passage of kidney stones. Stone root, for example, has natural diuretic properties that can help increase urine production and flush out kidney stones.  Lastly, people with OAB may experience incontinence, which can be caused by weakened pelvic muscles. Herbs like horsetail and saw palmetto can help strengthen pelvic muscles and improve bladder control. Horsetail, in particular, is rich in silica, which can help improve muscle tone and reduce incontinence.  These medicinal herbs can be consumed as teas, capsules, or tinctures, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
